CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-26-2026 Continued To May-27-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Dismissal Of Entire Action; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Apr-10-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Dameta O'Brien's unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Entire Action is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing consistent with Code of Civil Procedure 581(b) and Labor Code section 2699 and in light of the 10/9/2025 Order and Judgment in Galloway v. Medix Staffing Solutions, Inc., Los Angeles Superior Court Case No. 20STCV44053, the court authorizes Plaintiff's dismissal of her individual claims with prejudice and her representative PAGA claims without prejudice. Notably, on 11/1/2022, the court granted Plaintiff's motion to dismiss her class allegations and claims without prejudice.

Plaintiff must file a request for dismissal on the Judicial Council form and may include a copy of this order with that submission.

Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order consistent with the above and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-13-2022 Continued To Dec-14-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Medix Staffing Solutions, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion To Strike Paga Representative Allegations In Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-20-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Jacquelyne VanEmmerik, Esq. (213.232.3128; jacquelyne.vanemmerik@moonyanglaw.com) of Moon & Yang APC for Plaintiff.
Karimah Lamar, Esq. (619.232.0441; klamar@littler.com) of Littler Mendelson PC for Defendant.

The Court noted the contest to tentative ruling was untimely and allows oral arguments.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MEDIX STAFFING SOUTIONS, INC.'S Motion To Strike Paga Representative Allegations In Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint.

Defendant's (MEDIX STAFFING SOLUTIONS, INC.) motion to strike is denied.

Defendant urges this court to strike Plaintiff's representative claim under the Private Attorney General Act ("PAGA") in light of the United States Supreme Court's opinion in Viking River Cruises v. Moriana. Specifically, Defendant argues that Plaintiff loses standing to bring a representative PAGA claim after her individual PAGA claim has been separated. This same argument has been made frequently in this courthouse since the Viking River Cruises decision. Like the others, it too must be rejected.

In Kim v. Reins Int'l Corp., the California Supreme Court held: "Settlement of individual claims does not strip an aggrieved employee of standing, as the state's authorized representative, to pursue PAGA remedies." (Kim v. Reins Int'l California, Inc. (2020) 9 Cal.5th 73, 80.) The employer in Kim made the same argument as the Defendant here regarding statutory standing. The California Supreme Court rejected this argument, reasoning that to lose PAGA standing after settling individual claims is "at odds with the language of the statute, the statutory purpose supporting PAGA claims, and the overall statutory scheme." (Id. at 84.)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
